method_summary: The paper converts the preference poisoning problem into a structured binary sparse approximation problem and develops two attack methods: Binary-Aware Lattice Attack (BAL-A) using lattice reduction algorithms and Binary Matching Pursuit Attack (BMP-A) adapting binary matching pursuit to non-normalized gradient dictionaries.
key_innovation: The conversion of the preference poisoning problem into a structured binary sparse approximation problem and the development of two novel attack methods (BAL-A and BMP-A) that efficiently solve this problem.
title: Efficient Preference Poisoning Attack on Offline RLHF
